Adds examples to `np.char` module. Part of https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/21351.

Examples added:
* `array` - defchararray module
* ~~`isalpha` - ufunc_docstrings module~~
* `isspace` - ufunc_docstrings module
* ~~`mod` - strings module~~
* ~~`rfind` - strings module~~
* `splitlines` - strings module  but in char namespace 
* ~~`startswith` - strings module - example similar to `endswith`~~

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27318","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27318/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27318/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27318/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27318","id":2498658272,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86U7n_g","number":27318,"title":"TYP: ma.ones_like and ma.zeros_like not found by mypy because missing in ma/__init__.pyi","user":{"login":"sebschub","id":464150,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ2NDE1MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/464150?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/sebschub","html_url":"https://github.com/sebschub","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sebschub/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sebschub/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sebschub/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sebschub/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sebschub/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sebschub/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sebschub/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sebschub/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sebschub/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":2124388074,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyMTI0Mzg4MDc0","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/Static%20typing","name":"Static typing","color":"fcff6b","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-08-31T06:04:58Z","updated_at":"2024-09-02T01:26:55Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

mypy produces for both, `ma.ones_like` and `ma.zeros_like` the `error: Module has no attribute`. The apparent reason is that both are not listed in `ma/__init__.pyi`. Is this a simple omission? `ma/core.pyi` includes both, though, so `ma.core.ones_like` or `ma.core.zeros_like` work.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np
import numpy.ma as ma
import numpy.ma.core as mac

x: ma.MaskedArray = ma.MaskedArray([1.0, 3.0, 5.0])
# x = ma.MaskedArray([1.0, 3.0, 5.0])
print(ma.ones_like(x))
print(ma.zeros_like(x))

print(np.ones_like(x))

print(mac.ones_like(x))
print(mac.zeros_like(x))
```


### Error message:

```shell
test.py:7: error: Module has no attribute "ones_like"  [attr-defined]
test.py:8: error: Module has no attribute "zeros_like"  [attr-defined]
Found 2 errors in 1 file (checked 1 source file)
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

2.1.0
3.12.5 (main, Aug  7 2024, 00:00:00) [GCC 14.2.1 20240801 (Red Hat 14.2.1-1)]


### Type-checker version and settings:

mypy 1.11.2 (compiled: yes)

### Additional typing packages.

#### Reference issue
<!--Example: Closes gh-WXYZ.-->
Issue: https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/8319
Closes PR https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/8213

#### What does this implement/fix?
<!--Please explain your changes.-->
Rename `sosfreqz` to `freqz_sos` and keeps `sosfreqz` as an alias.

#### Additional information
<!--Any additional information you think is important.-->
I'd like to discuss three things:
1. Should I place `sosfreqz` in the [signa index](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/signal.html)? I think that the docs build fails if it's not there.
2. I also placed a message just like the one at the end of [interpolate](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/interpolate.html) for `pchip`. Is it necessary?

https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/776e64056f64bd70b503b7f8deb31fc155ac5c40/scipy/interpolate/__init__.py#L164-L165

3. From #8213:

Pandas version checks

- [X] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
>>> s = pd.Series([np.array([1, 2])])
>>> s
0    [1, 2]
dtype: object 
>>> s.to_markdown()

Traceback (most recent call last):
File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
File "/home/amu4ca/repo/venvs/sentinela-studies-main/lib/python3.10/site-packages/pandas/core/series.py", line 1970, in to_markdown
  return self.to_frame().to_markdown(
File "/home/amu4ca/repo/venvs/sentinela-studies-main/lib/python3.10/site-packages/pandas/util/_decorators.py", line 333, in wrapper
  return func(*args, **kwargs)
File "/home/amu4ca/repo/venvs/sentinela-studies-main/lib/python3.10/site-packages/pandas/core/frame.py", line 2984, in to_markdown
  result = tabulate.tabulate(self, **kwargs)
File "/home/amu4ca/repo/venvs/sentinela-studies-main/lib/python3.10/site-packages/tabulate/__init__.py", line 2048, in tabulate
  list_of_lists, headers = _normalize_tabular_data(
File "/home/amu4ca/repo/venvs/sentinela-studies-main/lib/python3.10/site-packages/tabulate/__init__.py", line 1471, in _normalize_tabular_data
  rows = list(map(lambda r: r if _is_separating_line(r) else list(r), rows))
File "/home/amu4ca/repo/venvs/sentinela-studies-main/lib/python3.10/site-packages/tabulate/__init__.py", line 1471, in <lambda>
  rows = list(map(lambda r: r if _is_separating_line(r) else list(r), rows))
File "/home/amu4ca/repo/venvs/sentinela-studies-main/lib/python3.10/site-packages/tabulate/__init__.py", line 107, in _is_separating_line
  (len(row) >= 1 and row[0] == SEPARATING_LINE)
ValueError: The truth value of an array with more than one element is ambiguous. Use a.any() or a.all() 
```


### Issue Description

When trying to convert a `Series` or a `DataFrame` to markdown with `np.ndarray`s as values an error is raised.


### Expected Behavior

The method should print something similar to `list`s behavior:
```python
>>> pd.Series([[1, 2]]).to_markdown()  # All Good!
'|    | 0      |\n|---:|:-------|\n|  0 | [1, 2] |' 
```

Perhaps any iterable should be converted to a list before sending to `tabulate`.

#### Reference issue
<!--Example: Closes gh-WXYZ.-->
Continues https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/8213 and https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/21025

#### What does this implement/fix?
<!--Please explain your changes.-->
Renames: 
 * `sosfilt` to `filt_sos`
 * `sosfilt_zi` to `filt_zi_sos`
 * `sosfiltfilt` to `filtfilt_sos`

Adds one backwards compatibility test for each function.

The one difference to what's been done with `sosfreqz` (https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/21025) is that this PR haven't touched the release notes. Other that that they are very similar.

#### Additional information
<!--Any additional information you think is important.-->
The motivation for this change is that this naming does not follows other function naming standards

> https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/8213#issue-281608289
> The name `sosfreqz` is inconsistent with `freqz_zpk`, `lp2lp_zpk`, etc.
> 
> The function's purpose (`freqz`) should be the first part of the name, so that all variants come up during tab completion.
> 
> `sosfreqz` is left as an explanatory alias function for backwards compatibility.
> 
> `sosfilt` `sosfilt_zi` `sosfiltfilt` should also be renamed by the same logic?

Feature Type

- [X] Adding new functionality to pandas

- [ ] Changing existing functionality in pandas

- [ ] Removing existing functionality in pandas


### Problem Description

Many times I wanted to convert a float column into percentage but it's very verbose. Adding a `str.format` method would make it easy to convert any numeric columns to percentage and also allow for many other use cases.

### Feature Description

```python
    # Series.str class
    def format(self, format_spec: str):
        return self.apply(lambda x: format_spec.format(x))

series.str.format("{:%}")
```

### Alternative Solutions

```python
series.apply(lambda x: "{:%}".format(x))
```

### Additional Context

There's many alternatives for creating formatted strings, but this feature should add the possibility to store the formatted values in `pd.Series` instead.

Feature Type

- [X] Adding new functionality to pandas

- [ ] Changing existing functionality in pandas

- [ ] Removing existing functionality in pandas


### Problem Description

Many times I wanted to convert a float column into percentage but it's very verbose. Adding a `str.format` method would make it easy to convert any numeric columns to percentage and also allow for many other use cases.

### Feature Description

```python
    # Series.str class
    def format(self, format_spec: str):
        return self.apply(lambda x: format_spec.format(x))

series.str.format("{:%}")
```

### Alternative Solutions

```python
series.apply(lambda x: "{:%}".format(x))
```

### Additional Context

There's many alternatives for creating formatted strings, but this feature should add the possibility to store the formatted values in `pd.Series` instead.

### Issue Description

When trying to convert a `Series` or a `DataFrame` to markdown with `np.ndarray`s as values an error is raised.


### Expected Behavior

The method should print something similar to `list`s behavior:
```python
>>> pd.Series([[1, 2]]).to_markdown()  # All Good!
'|    | 0      |\n|---:|:-------|\n|  0 | [1, 2] |' 
```

Perhaps any iterable should be converted to a list before sending to `tabulate`.

Feature Type

- [X] Adding new functionality to pandas

- [ ] Changing existing functionality in pandas

- [ ] Removing existing functionality in pandas


### Problem Description

Many times I wanted to convert a float column into percentage but it's very verbose. Adding a `str.format` method would make it easy to convert any numeric columns to percentage and also allow for many other use cases.

### Feature Description

```python
    # Series.str class
    def format(self, format_spec: str):
        return self.apply(lambda x: format_spec.format(x))

series.str.format("{:%}")
```

### Alternative Solutions

```python
series.apply(lambda x: "{:%}".format(x))
```

### Additional Context

There's many alternatives for creating formatted strings, but this feature should add the possibility to store the formatted values in `pd.Series` instead.

### np (12)
- [x] amax - Alias of max. Are examples needed?
- [x] amin - Alias of min
- [x] around - Alias of round
- [ ] getbufsize
- [ ] matrix_transpose
- [ ] setbufsize
- [ ] show_config
- [ ] show_runtime
- [ ] unique_all
- [ ] unique_counts
- [ ] unique_inverse
- [ ] unique_values
### np.char (7)
- [ ] array
- [ ] isalpha
- [ ] isspace
- [ ] mod
- [ ] rfind
- [ ] splitlines
- [ ] startswith
### np.ctypeslib (4)
- [ ] as_array
- [ ] as_ctypes
- [ ] as_ctypes_type
- [ ] load_library
### np.lib (2)
- [ ] add_docstring
- [ ] add_newdoc
### np.linalg (10)
- [ ] cross
- [ ] diagonal
- [ ] matmul
- [ ] matrix_norm
- [ ] matrix_transpose
- [ ] outer
- [ ] svdvals
- [ ] trace
- [ ] vecdot
- [ ] vector_norm
### np.ma (6)
- [ ] convolve
- [ ] correlate
- [ ] left_shift
- [ ] put
- [ ] reshape
- [ ] take
### np.rec (1)
- [ ] find_duplicate

As seen in #26623, if a library itself has a dot, e.g., `-lMAPL.base`, then the meson backend produces code like:
```meson
MAPL.base = declare_dependency(link_args : ['-lMAPL.base'])
```
But meson apparently sees `MAPL.base` as saying this is the `base` method in the `MAPL` type (or class?). Thus, it expects parentheses. 

So this PR uses a `replace()` call to replace all the `.` to `_` in the `lib` identifier. This has to be done both in the `declare_dependency` and in the `dependencies` bit of `py.extension_module`.

With this fix I get:
```meson
MAPL_constants = declare_dependency(link_args : ['-lMAPL.constants'])
MAPL_base = declare_dependency(link_args : ['-lMAPL.base'])
```

This seems to fix any issues in my testing, but my fear is cases I don't have. (Though perhaps libraries with dots is a rare case anyway and mainly a style used in the our code.)

Closes #26623 

---

NOTE: I tested this with Numpy 1.26.4, and I see main is now in v2 land. It would be nice to get this into a v1 tag as well if v2 is still a ways off. I could backport this to `maintenance/1.26.x` if desired.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26634/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26634/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2152540797","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/26634#issuecomment-2152540797","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26634","id":2152540797,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86ATSp9","user":{"login":"luxedo","id":14118472,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTE4NDcy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14118472?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo","html_url":"https://github.com/luxedo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-06T13:39:01Z","updated_at":"2024-06-06T13:39:01Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Yes! Also check https://github.com/scipy/scipy.org"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-06-07T16:35:48Z","updated_at":"2024-06-07T18:33:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Quite a few docstring examples in scipy.stats show an incorrect repr of `*Result` classes. Consider the docstring of `ttest_ind` (https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.stats.ttest_ind.html#scipy.stats.ttest_ind):

```
In [15]: import numpy as np
    ...: from scipy import stats
    ...: rng = np.random.default_rng()

In [16]: rvs1 = stats.norm.rvs(loc=5, scale=10, size=500, random_state=rng)
    ...: rvs2 = stats.norm.rvs(loc=5, scale=10, size=500, random_state=rng)
    ...: stats.ttest_ind(rvs1, rvs2)
Out[16]: TtestResult(statistic=0.40375493221427916, pvalue=0.6864794357079085, df=998.0)
```

Note that `TtestResult` has three keys (`statistic`, `pvalue` and `df`) while the docstring example only shows two, `statistics` and `pvalue`.

Yes, `TtestResult` unpacks into these two values for backwards compatibility. 

So we need to either adjust the repr or fix the docstrings.

This was not caught by the smoke-docs machinery because of a bug which just got fixed in https://github.com/scipy/scipy_doctest/pull/162 by @luxedo. The fix will be available in the upcoming scipy-doctest==1.2.0 release relatively soon.

Meanwhile to see the failures locally, do 

```
$ # clone scipy-doctest & cd 
$ pip install -e .
$ cd path/to/scipy
$ python dev.py smoke-docs -s stats
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/20921/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/20921/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/comments/2155329663","html_url":"https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/20921#issuecomment-2155329663","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/20921","id":2155329663,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABZIoc6Ad7h_","user":{"login":"luxedo","id":14118472,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTE4NDcy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14118472?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo","html_url":"https://github.com/luxedo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-07T18:33:29Z","updated_at":"2024-06-07T18:33:29Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"[KstestResult](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.stats.kstest.html) has the following attributes:
* statisticfloat
* pvalue
* statistic_location
* statistic_signint

[TtestResult](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.stats._result_classes.TtestResult.html) has the following attributes:
* statistic
* pvalue
* df

I`m adding them to the doctests because they are not private attributes.

There's also an issue with the return type for [ttest_ind](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.stats.ttest_ind.html):
```
Expected:
    Ttest_indResult(statistic=-0.4390847099199348, pvalue=0.6606952038870015)
Got:
    TtestResult(statistic=-0.4390847099199348, pvalue=0.6606952038870015, df=998.0)
```
The documentation says that `ttest_ind` returns a `TtestResult` not a `Ttest_indResult`. So I think I can update as well. 

Is it possible that this mismatch in return types is happening to other doctests?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/comments/2155329663/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

When creating an `ndarray` from a generator the generator is not consumed:

```python
>>>: np.array(x for x in [3, 2, 1])
array(<generator object <genexpr> at 0x7663dc12f9f0>, dtype=object)
```

By wrapping the list in the generator it is consumed and creates the list:

```python
>>> np.array([x for x in [3, 2, 1]])
array([3, 2, 1])
>>> np.array(list(x for x in [3, 2, 1])) 
array([3, 2, 1])
```

In fact, it is possible to wrap pretty much anything in an `ndarray`. I don't know if it is expected or useful to do so: 

```python
>>> np.array(tuple)
array(<class 'tuple'>, dtype=object)
>>> np.array(print)
array(<built-in function print>, dtype=object)
>>> np.array({})
array({}, dtype=object)
```

I see that it seems like a confusing behavior but the [documentation](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.array.html) does not specify that it supports iterables or generators, only _array_like_.

I propose that the documentation should have a better definition for what's _array_like_. There's already [some discussion](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40378427/numpy-formal-definition-of-array-like-objects) about that.

There are 3 tests:

### The first regression test passes:
https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/5aaede6eef94b35b926937129a4bff785d020269/numpy/_core/tests/test_regression.py#L859-L862

### The second test passes too:
https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/5aaede6eef94b35b926937129a4bff785d020269/numpy/_core/tests/test_regression.py#L2017-L2020
The new verification does not catches the error and the expected exception is thrown in a `getattr` inside `_wrapit`:
```python
File ~/code/os/numpy/numpy/_core/fromnumeric.py:46, in _wrapit(obj, method, *args, **kwds)
     43 # As this already tried the method, subok is maybe quite reasonable here
     44 # but this follows what was done before. TODO: revisit this.
     45 arr, = conv.as_arrays(subok=False)
---> 46 result = getattr(arr, method)(*args, **kwds)
     48 return conv.wrap(result, to_scalar=False)

TypeError: '<' not supported between instances of 'tuple' and 'float'
```

### The third test fails ([logs](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/actions/runs/9439431789/job/25997548313?pr=26650#step:9:772)) and is related to https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/642 and https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/2658.

https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/5aaede6eef94b35b926937129a4bff785d020269/numpy/_core/tests/test_regression.py#L2069-L2078

Which now throws:
```python
ValueError: Incompatible types for searching: a ((numpy.record, [('f0', '<i4'), ('f1', '<i4')])) and v (int64)
```

 
## Summary
There's already a rudimentary type verification by means of not being able to compare types, but is not enough to catch `string` and `integer` discrepancies.

With this PR there will be two types of errors: 
1. The old `TypeError` by type comparison: `TypeError: '<' not supported between instances of 'tuple' and 'float'`
2. The new `ValueError` given `resolve_dtypes`: `TypeError: Incompatible types for searching: a (str) and v (int64)`

My suggestion from https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/26650/commits/647bb8583120a2513f57e454c86bb5a329fcf0c6 is to just use `TypeError` for the new exception as well, which I think makes sense. But I'm a bit bothered by the fact that there are two exceptions for handling incompatible data types.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2157023983/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26649","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26649/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26649/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26649/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/26649","id":2342364730,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86LnaY6","number":26649,"title":"DOC: Add CI to commit messages standard acronyms","user":{"login":"luxedo","id":14118472,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTE4NDcy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14118472?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo","html_url":"https://github.com/luxedo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359990,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5OTkw","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/04%20-%20Documentation","name":"04 - Documentation","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-06-09T16:17:08Z","updated_at":"2024-06-10T12:46:04Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Issue with current documentation:

The current [commit messages acronyms](https://numpy.org/devdocs/dev/development_workflow.html#writing-the-commit-message) does not contain `CI`:

```
API: an (incompatible) API change
BENCH: changes to the benchmark suite
BLD: change related to building numpy
BUG: bug fix
DEP: deprecate something, or remove a deprecated object
DEV: development tool or utility
DOC: documentation
ENH: enhancement
MAINT: maintenance commit (refactoring, typos, etc.)
MNT: alias for MAINT
REL: related to releasing numpy
REV: revert an earlier commit
STY: style fix (whitespace, PEP8)
TST: addition or modification of tests
TYP: static typing
WIP: work in progress, do not merge
```

Even though there's almost 300 commits that uses this acronym:

```bash
$ git log | grep "^ \+CI:" | wc -l
293
```

The current [commit messages acronyms](https://numpy.org/devdocs/dev/development_workflow.html#writing-the-commit-message) does not contain `CI`:

```
API: an (incompatible) API change
BENCH: changes to the benchmark suite
BLD: change related to building numpy
BUG: bug fix
DEP: deprecate something, or remove a deprecated object
DEV: development tool or utility
DOC: documentation
ENH: enhancement
MAINT: maintenance commit (refactoring, typos, etc.)
MNT: alias for MAINT
REL: related to releasing numpy
REV: revert an earlier commit
STY: style fix (whitespace, PEP8)
TST: addition or modification of tests
TYP: static typing
WIP: work in progress, do not merge
```

Even though there's almost 300 commits that uses this acronym:

```bash
$ git log | grep "^ \+CI:" | wc -l
293
```

Adds examples to `np.char` module. Part of https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/21351.

Examples added:
* `array` - defchararray module
* `isalpha` - ufunc_docstrings module
* `isspace` - ufunc_docstrings module
* `mod` - strings module
* `rfind` - strings module
* `splitlines` - strings module  but in char namespace 
* `startswith` - strings module - example similar to `endswith`

When passing list as arguments to `np.linalg.cross` an unexpected `AttributeError` exception is raised.


The [documentation](https://numpy.org/devdocs/reference/generated/numpy.linalg.cross.html) is clear about the types for the input, which should be _array_like_

> _x1: array_like_
> _x2: array_like_

The exception is thrown at a shape validation for the inputs, which treats them as `ndarrays`:

```python
def cross(...):
    if x1.shape[axis] != 3 or x2.shape[axis] != 3:
        raise ValueError(
            "Both input arrays must be (arrays of) 3-dimensional vectors, "
            f"but they are {x1.shape[axis]} and {x2.shape[axis]} "
            "dimensional instead."
        )

    return _core_cross(x1, x2, axis=axis)
```

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
np.linalg.cross([1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6])
```


### Error message:

```shell
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[5], line 1
----> 1 np.linalg.cross(x, y)

File ~/code/os/numpy/numpy/linalg/_linalg.py:3233, in cross(x1, x2, axis)
   3139 @array_function_dispatch(_cross_dispatcher)
   3140 def cross(x1, x2, /, *, axis=-1):
   3141     """
   3142     Returns the cross product of 3-element vectors.
   3143 
   (...)
   3231 
   3232     """
-> 3233     if x1.shape[axis] != 3 or x2.shape[axis] != 3:
   3234         raise ValueError(
   3235             "Both input arrays must be (arrays of) 3-dimensional vectors, "
   3236             f"but they are {x1.shape[axis]} and {x2.shape[axis]} "
   3237             "dimensional instead."
   3238         )
   3240     return _core_cross(x1, x2, axis=axis)

AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'shape'
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

```
2.1.0.dev0+git20240612.86c7f4b
3.11.8 (main, Feb 12 2024, 14:50:05) [GCC 13.2.1 20230801]
```

Describe your issue.

When trying to calculate the cosine distance between `[1+2j, 3+4j]` and `[5 + 6j, 7+8j]` scipy raises a `ValueError`.
[WolframAlpha](https://www.wolframalpha.com/input?i2d=true&i=CosineDistance%5C%2891%29%7B1%2B2i%2C+3%2B4i%7D%5C%2844%29+%7B5+%2B+6i%2C7%2B8i%7D%5C%2893%29) gives a solution:

![image](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/assets/14118472/fe011308-3259-4080-be48-6c988c183d87)


### Reproducing Code Example

```python
from scipy.spatial.distance import cosine
cosine([1+2j, 3+4j], [5+6j, 7+8j])
```


### Error message

```shell
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ValueError                                Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[14], line 1
----> 1 cosine([1+2j, 3+4j], [5+6j, 7+8j])

File /usr/lib/python3.11/site-packages/scipy/spatial/distance.py:695, in cosine(u, v, w)
    653 """
    654 Compute the Cosine distance between 1-D arrays.
    655 
   (...)
    690 
    691 """
    692 # cosine distance is also referred to as 'uncentered correlation',
    693 #   or 'reflective correlation'
    694 # clamp the result to 0-2
--> 695 return max(0, min(correlation(u, v, w=w, centered=False), 2.0))

File /usr/lib/python3.11/site-packages/scipy/spatial/distance.py:647, in correlation(u, v, w, centered)
    645 uu = np.dot(u, uw)
    646 vv = np.dot(v, vw)
--> 647 dist = 1.0 - uv / math.sqrt(uu * vv)
    648 # Return absolute value to avoid small negative value due to rounding
    649 return abs(dist)

ValueError: math domain error
```

#### Reference issue
<!--Example: Closes gh-WXYZ.-->
Issue: https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/9961
Closes https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/10102 too.
#### What does this implement/fix?
<!--Please explain your changes.-->

Moves `spatial.distance` casting rules implemented in https://github.com/scipy/scipy/commit/05378cb0c056c013cc4403c6deb8f070426d159f to `_validate_vector`.

Adds classes for unittesting: `TestDtypeStability` and `TestFloatCasting`.

#### Additional information
<!--Any additional information you think is important.-->

I'd like a little feedback before continuing. I want to implement tests for all functions in the `spatial.distance` module by using Pytest's parametrize decorator. I just want to know if the classes `TestDtypeStability` and `TestFloatCasting` makes sense for testing all functions and if the check in `_validate_vector` is correct.

I also don't have all the casting rules. For example:
```
cosine(Any, Any) => float64
correlation(Any, Any) => float64
hamming(uint8, uint8) => uint8  # What about overflow?
hamming(char, char) => uint64
seuclidean(uint8, uint8) => float64
```

#### Reference issue
<!--Example: Closes gh-WXYZ.-->
Issue: https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/8319
Closes PR https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/8213

#### What does this implement/fix?
<!--Please explain your changes.-->
Rename `sosfreqz` to `freqz_sos` and keeps `sosfreqz` as an alias.

#### Additional information
<!--Any additional information you think is important.-->
I'd like to discuss three things:
1. Should I place `sosfreqz` in the [signa index](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/signal.html)? I think that the docs build fails if it's not there.
2. I also placed a message just like the one at the end of [interpolate](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/interpolate.html) for `pchip`. Is it necessary.
3. From #8213:
> `sosfilt` `sosfilt_zi` `sosfiltfilt` should also be renamed by the same logic?

As soon as I have some feedback I wrap up the PR for merging.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/pulls/comments/1650261488","pull_request_review_id":2134510353,"id":1650261488,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABZIoc5iXP3w","diff_hunk":"@@ -834,6 +835,248 @@ def remez(numtaps, bands, desired, *, weight=None, type='bandpass',
                             maxiter, grid_density)
 
 
+def _oddround(x):
+    """Return the nearest odd integer from x."""
+
+    return x - np.mod(x, 2) + 1
+
+
+def _oddceil(x):
+    """Return the smallest odd integer not less than x."""
+
+    return _oddround(x + 1)
+
+
+def _remlplen_herrmann(fp, fs, dp, ds):
+    """
+    Determine the length of the low pass filter with passband frequency
+    fp, stopband frequency fs, passband ripple dp, and stopband ripple ds.
+    fp and fs must be normalized with respect to the sampling frequency.
+    Note that the filter order is one less than the filter length.
+
+    Uses approximation algorithm described by Herrmann et al.:
+    """
+
+    dF = fs - fp
+    a = [5.309e-3, 7.114e-2, -4.761e-1, -2.66e-3, -5.941e-1, -4.278e-1]
+    b = [11.01217, 0.51244]
+    Dinf = (
+        np.log10(ds) * (a[0] * np.log10(dp) ** 2 + a[1] * np.log10(dp) + a[2])
+        + a[3] * np.log10(dp) ** 2
+        + a[4] * np.log10(dp)
+        + a[5]
+    )
+    f = b[0] + b[1] * (np.log10(dp) - np.log10(ds))
+    N1 = Dinf / dF - f * dF + 1
+
+    return int(_oddround(N1))
+
+
+def _remlplen_kaiser(fp, fs, dp, ds):
+    """
+    Determine the length of the low pass filter with passband frequency
+    fp, stopband frequency fs, passband ripple dp, and stopband ripple ds.
+    fp and fs must be normalized with respect to the sampling frequency.
+    Note that the filter order is one less than the filter length.
+
+    Uses approximation algorithm described by Kaiser:
+    """
+
+    dF = fs - fp
+    N2 = (-20 * np.log10(np.sqrt(dp * ds)) - 13.0) / (14.6 * dF) + 1.0
+
+    return int(_oddceil(N2))
+
+
+def _remlplen_ichige(fp, fs, dp, ds):
+    """
+    Determine the length of the low pass filter with passband frequency
+    fp, stopband frequency fs, passband ripple dp, and stopband ripple ds.
+    fp and fs must be normalized with respect to the sampling frequency.
+    Note that the filter order is one less than the filter length.
+
+    Uses approximation algorithm described by Ichige et al.
+    """
+    dF = fs - fp
+
+    def v(dF, dp):
+        return 2.325 * ((-np.log10(dp)) ** -0.445) * dF ** (-1.39)
+
+    def arctan_g(fp, dF, d):
+        return (2.0 / np.pi) * np.arctan(
+            v(dF, dp) * (1.0 / fp - 1.0 / (0.5 - dF))
+        )
+
+    def arctan_h(fp, dF, c):
+        return (2.0 / np.pi) * np.arctan(
+            (c / dF) * (1.0 / fp - 1.0 / (0.5 - dF))
+        )
+
+    Nc = ceil(1.0 + (1.101 / dF) * (-np.log10(2.0 * dp)) ** 1.1)
+    Nm = (0.52 / dF) * np.log10(dp / ds) * (-np.log10(dp)) ** 0.17
+    N3 = ceil(
+        Nc
+        * (arctan_g(fp, dF, dp) + arctan_g(0.5 - dF - fp, dF, dp) + 1.0)
+        / 3.0
+    )
+    DN = ceil(
+        Nm
+        * (
+            arctan_h(fp, dF, 1.1)
+            - (arctan_h(0.5 - dF - fp, dF, 0.29) - 1.0) / 2.0
+        )
+    )
+    N4 = N3 + DN
+
+    return int(N4)
+
+
+def remezord(freqs, amps, rips, fs=1.0, alg="ichige"):
+    """
+    Filter parameter selection for the Remez exchange algorithm.
+
+    Calculate the parameters required by the Remez exchange algorithm to
+    construct a finite impulse response (FIR) filter that approximately meets
+    the specified design.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    freqs : array_like
+        A monotonic sequence of non-negative band edges in Hertz. All elements
+        must be less than half the sampling frequency ``fs``.
+    amps : array_like
+        A sequence containing the desired amplitudes of the signal to be
+        filtered over the corresponding frequency bands in ``freqs``.
+    rips : array_like
+        A sequence specifying the maximum allowable ripples of each band in the
+        frequency response.
+    fs : float, optional
+        The sampling frequency of the signal. Defaults to 1.0
+    alg : {'herrmann', 'kaiser', 'ichige'}, optional
+        Filter length approximation algorithm. Possible algorithms are
+        Herrmann [1]_, Kaiser [2]_, or Ichige [3]_. (Default: 'ichige')
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    numtaps : int
+        The desired number of taps in the FIR filter.
+    bands : ndarray
+        A monotonic sequence containing the band edges.
+    desired : ndarray
+        A sequence half the size of bands containing the desired gain in each
+        of the specified bands.
+    weight : ndarray
+        A relative weighting to give to each band region.
+
+    Raises
+    ------
+    ValueError:
+        - If any element in ``freqs`` is negative, greater than 0.5, or the
+          length is not valid.
+        - If the lengths of ``amps`` and ``rips`` are not equal or one more
+          than half the length of ``freqs``.
+        - If any element in ``rips`` is negative.
+        - If ``alg`` is not a valid string option.
+
+    See Also
+    --------
+    remez
+
+    Notes
+    -----
+    .. versionadded:: 1.15.0
+
+    References
+    ----------
+    .. [1] O. Herrmann, L.R. Raviner, and D.S.K. Chan, Practical Design Rules
+           for Optimum Finite Impulse Response Low-Pass Digital Filters, Bell
+           Syst. Tech. Jour., 52(6):769-799, Jul./Aug. 1973.
+    .. [2] J.F. Kaiser, Nonrecursive Digital Filter Design Using I_0-sinh
+           Window function, Proc. IEEE Int. Symp. Circuits and Systems, 20-23,
+           April 1974.
+    .. [3] K. Ichige, M. Iwaki, and R. Ishii, Accurate Estimation of Minimum
+           Filter Length for Optimum FIR Digital Filters, IEEE Transactions on
+           Circuits and Systems, 47(10):1008-1017, October 2000.
+
+    Examples
+    --------
+    >>> import numpy as np
+    >>> from scipy.signal import remezord
+    >>> rp, rs = 3, 40  # Passband ripple, Stopband ripple
+    >>> fs = 2000  # Sampling frequency
+    >>> freqs = np.array([500, 600])  # Band edges
+    >>> amps = np.array([1, 0])  # Amplitudes
+    >>> rips = [(10**(rp/20)-1)/(10**(rp/20)+1), 10**(-rs/20)]  # Max ripples
+    >>> numtaps, bands, desired, weights = remezord(freqs, amps, rips, fs=fs)
+    >>> numtaps
+    27
+    >>> bands
+    [0, 0.25, 0.3, 0.5]
+    >>> desired
+    [1., 0.]
+    >>> weights

- [x] code needs unit tests.

- [x] the code needs some PEP8 cleanup (spaces around operators etc.)
- [x] the Hz parameter is missing from the docstring.
- [x] docstring format: See should be See Also, typo in Paramters, remove blank lines right below section headings.

#### Additional information

Tasks to complete this PR:

- [x] Lint: Remove lambdas
- [x] code needs unit tests.
- [x] all functions except remezord need to start with an underscore.
- [x] the docstrings for remlplen_* repeat the same text 3 times. The 2nd and 3rd docstring can refer to the first one. The references to papers should all be moved to the remezord docstring.
- [x] the code needs some PEP8 cleanup (spaces around operators etc.)
- [x] the Hz parameter is missing from the docstring.
- [x] docstring format: See should be See Also, typo in Paramters, remove blank lines right below section headings.

#### Reference issue
<!--Example: Closes gh-WXYZ.-->
Issue: https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/8319
Closes PR https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/8213

#### What does this implement/fix?
<!--Please explain your changes.-->
Rename `sosfreqz` to `freqz_sos` and keeps `sosfreqz` as an alias.

#### Additional information
<!--Any additional information you think is important.-->
I'd like to discuss three things:
1. Should I place `sosfreqz` in the [signa index](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/signal.html)? I think that the docs build fails if it's not there.
2. I also placed a message just like the one at the end of [interpolate](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/interpolate.html) for `pchip`. Is it necessary?

https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/776e64056f64bd70b503b7f8deb31fc155ac5c40/scipy/interpolate/__init__.py#L164-L165

3. From #8213:
> `sosfilt` `sosfilt_zi` `sosfiltfilt` should also be renamed by the same logic?

As soon as I have some feedback I wrap up the PR for merging.

#### Reference issue
<!--Example: Closes gh-WXYZ.-->
Issue: https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/8319
Closes PR https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/8213

#### What does this implement/fix?
<!--Please explain your changes.-->
Rename `sosfreqz` to `freqz_sos` and keeps `sosfreqz` as an alias.

#### Additional information
<!--Any additional information you think is important.-->
I'd like to discuss three things:
1. Should I place `sosfreqz` in the [signa index](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/signal.html)? I think that the docs build fails if it's not there.
2. I also placed a message just like the one at the end of [interpolate](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/interpolate.html) for `pchip`. Is it necessary?

https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/776e64056f64bd70b503b7f8deb31fc155ac5c40/scipy/interpolate/__init__.py#L164-L165

3. From #8213:
> `sosfilt` `sosfilt_zi` `sosfiltfilt` should also be renamed by the same logic?

As soon as I have some feedback I wrap up the PR for merging.

=====
numpy
=====

numpy.get_include
-----------------

```

I have found a little error in the NumPy documentation, version 1.26, NumPy for MATLAB users, Fundamental and usage, NumPy user guide. It locates as the picture below. [Website Link](https://numpy.org/doc/1.26/user/numpy-for-matlab-users.html)
![bug](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/assets/96516639/b8730348-84ad-46b6-92cd-3584ed0952bd)
The code "**rng.random(3, 4)**" is wrong, and should be corrected into "**rng.random((3, 4))**". A bracket is missed. I have just found it at about UTC 09:30, 5/20/2024.

### Idea or request for content:

```shell
Python 3.11.8 (main, Feb 12 2024, 14:50:05) [GCC 13.2.1 20230801] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> from numpy.random import default_rng
>>> rng = default_rng(42)
>>> rng.random(3, 4)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "_generator.pyx", line 289, in numpy.random._generator.Generator.random
TypeError: Cannot interpret '4' as a data type
>>> rng.random((3, 4))
array([[0.77395605, 0.43887844, 0.85859792, 0.69736803],
       [0.09417735, 0.97562235, 0.7611397 , 0.78606431],
       [0.12811363, 0.45038594, 0.37079802, 0.92676499]])
>>> 
```

The [documentation](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/random/generated/numpy.random.Generator.random.html) is very clear as well. It only receives a single argument for the shape (size) of the output.
```python

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26220","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26220/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26220/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26220/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/26220","id":2229682203,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86E5kAb","number":26220,"title":"ENH: np.roll: extra argument to pad with zeros or constant","user":{"login":"josephernest","id":6168083,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxNjgwODM=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6168083?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/josephernest","html_url":"https://github.com/josephernest","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/josephernest/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/josephernest/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/josephernest/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/josephernest/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/josephernest/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/josephernest/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/josephernest/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/josephernest/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/josephernest/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-04-07T10:23:24Z","updated_at":"2024-05-21T01:51:50Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Proposed new feature or change:

In many situations, it is required to shift an array (like `np.roll`) and fill the values with zeros, instead of making the array "loop". 

Is there a way to implement this:

    x = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    np.roll(x, 2, method="constant")   #  [ 0 0 1 2 3 ]   instead of  [ 4 5 1 2 3 ]
    np.roll(x, -2, method="constant")  #  [ 3 4 5 0 0 ]  instead of   [ 3 4 5 1 2 ]

?

Example : [python numpy roll with padding](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/2777907/python-numpy-roll-with-padding)

Important notes:

* solutions with `np.pad` exist but are slower, see https://stackoverflow.com/questions/2777907/python-numpy-roll-with-padding#comment3270288_3153267

* other people have the needs for this: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/2777907/python-numpy-roll-with-padding#comment120688507_2778068: *I think np.roll should do it natively with a keyword argument.* (quite upvoted)

* this solution works: https://stackoverflow.com/a/2778068 but it would be interesting for the Numpy API to have this useful option (in the same way `np.pad` has `constant` mode, etc.)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26220/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26220/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2121554999","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/26220#issuecomment-2121554999","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26220","id":2121554999,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP85-dFw3","user":{"login":"luxedo","id":14118472,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTE4NDcy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14118472?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo","html_url":"https://github.com/luxedo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-05-21T01:51:49Z","updated_at":"2024-05-21T01:51:49Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I disagree of this proposal. 

The [documentation](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.roll.html) states that:
> Roll array elements along a given axis.
>
> Elements that roll beyond the last position are re-introduced at the first.

Furthermore, I think that the name `roll` is very expressive in it's intent in my opinion.

I'd much prefer the creation of a new function like [scipy.ndimage.shift](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.ndimage.shift.html).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2121554999/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

The error occurs at `NX.asanyarray` and `NX.zeros_like` that casts python's `float` to `np.float64` instead of `np.float16`.
```python
    p = NX.asarray(p)
    if isinstance(x, poly1d):
        y = 0
    else:
        x = NX.asanyarray(x)
        y = NX.zeros_like(x)
    for pv in p:
        y = y * x + pv
    return y
```

One possible solution would be to test if `x` is a numpy array and pass the `dtype` argument.
```python
    p = NX.asarray(p)
    if isinstance(x, poly1d):
        y = 0
    elif isinstance(x, ndarray):  # Preserve dtypes for NEP 50
        x = NX.asanyarray(x, dtype=x.dtype)
        y = NX.zeros_like(x, dtype=x.dtype)
    else:
        x = NX.asanyarray(x)
        y = NX.zeros_like(x)
    for pv in p:
        y = y * x + pv
    return y
```

Or maybe `asanyarray` and `zeros_like` should return the type correctly. Fixes #26484

The error occurs because `NX.asanyarray` and `NX.zeros_like` that casts python's float to np.float64 instead of np.float16. This fix adds tests for builtin Python types to avoid those         PyErr_SetString(PyExc_ValueError, "Capsule must be named 'mem_handler'")
        return NULL;
    }

Tests capsule name and sets `PyErr` if not valid:
```c
    if (!PyCapsule_IsValid(handler, MEM_HANDLER_CAPSULE_NAME)) {
        PyErr_SetString(PyExc_ValueError, "Capsule must be named 'mem_handler'")
        return NULL;
    }

Running Mypy on some functions within `numpy.ma` lead to type checking errors due to missing annotations.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python

a = numpy.ma.array([1, 2, 3, 4], mask=[0, 0, 1, 0])
b = numpy.ma.masked_array([1, 2, 3, 4], mask=[0, 0, 1, 0])
```

```shell

### Context for the issue:

We rely on type checking to find bugs in our software that uses Numpy before it is executed. This enables higher code quality and reliability. False positive type errors cost time to track down and can distract from real issues.

Type annotations also help IDEs such as PyCharm and VS Code perform better intellisense during development.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26404/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26404/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2132470655","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/26404#issuecomment-2132470655","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/26404","id":2132470655,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP85_Gut_","user":{"login":"luxedo","id":14118472,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTE4NDcy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14118472?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo","html_url":"https://github.com/luxedo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/luxedo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-05-27T00:59:12Z","updated_at":"2024-05-27T00:59:12Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"## Typing `ma/core.pyi`

Some functions and methods have inconsistent array argument naming. For example:

[np.ma.min](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ma.min.html) => `obj`
[np.min](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.min.html) => `a`
[np.flip](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.flip.html) => `m`

I think that some different names might make sense, like `m` for a mask. But I haven't searched extensively.

Tests capsule name and sets `PyErr` if not valid:
```c
    if (!PyCapsule_IsValid(handler, MEM_HANDLER_CAPSULE_NAME)) {
        PyErr_SetString(PyExc_ValueError, "Capsule must be named 'mem_handler'")
        return NULL;
    }

Closes https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/26137.

Tests capsule name and sets `PyErr` if not valid:
```c
    if (!PyCapsule_IsValid(handler, MEM_HANDLER_CAPSULE_NAME)) {
        PyErr_SetString(PyExc_ValueError, "Capsule must be named 'mem_handler'")
        return NULL;
    }

Closes https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/26137.

Tests capsule name and sets `PyErr` if not valid:
```c
    if (!PyCapsule_IsValid(handler, MEM_HANDLER_CAPSULE_NAME)) {
        PyErr_SetString(PyExc_ValueError, "Capsule must be named 'mem_handler'")
        return NULL;
    }

Closes https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/26137.

Tests capsule name and sets `PyErr` if not valid:
```c
    if (!PyCapsule_IsValid(handler, MEM_HANDLER_CAPSULE_NAME)) {
        PyErr_SetString(PyExc_ValueError, "Capsule must be named 'mem_handler'")
        return NULL;
    }
